title: More Tools+ by Anoosh
description: Adds new tool sets from Amethyst, Coal, Echo Shards, Emerald, Redstone, Quartz, and Prismarine — each with unique traits and balanced progression.
body:
More Tools is a small, focused mod that expands Minecraft’s tool progression by adding full tool sets crafted from a handful of new and under-used materials. Each set is designed to feel distinct — different look, different niche — while staying balanced for survival play. Expect handy mid- to late-game options (and a few fun utility quirks) that encourage exploration and resource variety without breaking progression.

About the author — Anoosh
Hi — I’m Anoosh, a Minecraft modder who loves making small, polished additions that actually feel useful in survival. I build with balance in mind: clear tradeoffs, simple crafting, and a little bit of personality for each item. Feedback and localization help are welcome — drop bug reports or suggestions on the mod page.

Amethyst Tools
Shimmering violet tools with slightly increased enchantability and a mellow sound when used. They’re not the strongest, but they get better enchantments more reliably and look great in crystal-themed builds.

![1](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b40df654d48483ab70e1767b9c79bac365116f59.png)

Coal (Char) Tools
Budget starter tools you can craft early with coal/charcoal as a partial substitute for wood/stone. Cheap to make and quick to replace — perfect for mining runs when you don’t want to waste higher-tier materials.

![2](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f4e6a782ff27010c0d28428f840c10657c00f49f.png)

Echo Shard Tools
Rare, mysterious tools made from Echo Shards. Designed for utility: short, cooldown-based “echo” effects (think tiny blink or brief item-pull) and a unique visual/sonic flair. Great for exploration and emergency escapes.

![3](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/554e288a4ff9a63918d5d6ac1d2bef030e81d96e.png)

Emerald Tools
Trader’s tools that emphasize luck and enchantability. These tools have better fortune/loot synergy and are easier to get good enchantments on — ideal for players who focus on trading and resource farming.

![4](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/47799e71204ee1777039c35241eceb5706860153.png)

Redstone Tools
Mechanized, utility-focused tools that incorporate short bursts of speed or powered behavior (for example: momentary mining speed boosts or redstone-style active abilities). Excellent for builders and players who like techy, fast workflows.

![5](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b14791698580434b20441cd77a2ba6a8261c5a96.png)

Quartz Tools
Precision tools that favor speed and responsiveness. Slightly higher attack/swing speed or tool responsiveness, making them a favorite for fast building and precise work rather than raw durability.

![12](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f943fb2350d7988f90cbf1d62d514b2aa5efc1c8.png)

Prismarine Tools
Aquatic tools tailored for underwater use: faster underwater mining/harvesting, better durability when wet, and simple repair synergy with prismarine shards. Perfect for ocean explorers and underwater base builders.

![213](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/080b70991308580c3b6b96c3c5558a8a0eb51161.png)
